conversations.say: Morgan, our records confirm multiple collectors and changing routines across the years, but you must continue to respect customer privacy and attribution limits as agreed.
conversations.offer: Confirmation of ledger continuity and collector variations.
conversations.request: Strict adherence to our privacy and attribution agreement.

conversations.say: Inez, we have held the line against outside exploitation of our local records and informal neighborhood arrangements.
conversations.offer: Mutual defense of local customer and worker protections.
conversations.request: Your continued support as we finalize our public stance.

conversations.say: Dee, our shift logs and service exit timings correctly reflect ordinary neighborhood operations rather than any single suspect.
conversations.offer: Shared context on service exit rhythms.

public_statement: My counter records show multiple collectors and changing routines over the years, not a single static ghost. We protect our customers and reject manufactured certainty.

commitments.promise: Protect local customer records and informal arrangements from public exposure
Restrict ledger disclosure terms and avoid naming any customer as R.

correction: Clarifying that multiple collectors and shared accounts used the R-associated services across years, which complicates any simple single-author narrative.
goal_progress: Maintained strict customer privacy and attribution limits with Morgan.
Upheld local protections and shared boundaries with Inez and Dee.
Resolved the final reconstruction ballot based on shared accounts and changing routines.
reasoning_summary: Rafi Calderón concludes Section 3 by honoring neighborhood privacy commitments with Inez and Morgan, reinforcing the reality of multiple collectors and shared routines over the years, and completing the final ballot.
